Utilizing our high-impact steel wire soil sifting pans is one of the first steps in successful gold prospecting. The Classifier Soil Sifting Screens will aid your recovery efforts by separating unwanted coarse raw material into finer, manageable good material ready to wash in your gold pan or run through a sluice box. Each soil classifying screen will fit over a standard 5 gallon bucket as well as a 14" gold pan and they are stackable, interlocking on top of each other to ensure that they will not slip or slide when used in conjunction with one another. Proper classification of material by size is the key to more efficient gold separation. This is true for roughing out your material, all the way to the final step of fine gold separation. Using a gold sifter screen is essential for gold panning, sluicing or running a high banker well. Separating and processing your gold concentrates by size with a classifier makes gold recovery much more efficient and less labor intensive, is faster and a more effective, resulting in more gold!

title: ToolTreaux Safety 18k Gold Testing Kit, 7pc
short_description: Safely test the karat content of gold with ToolTreaux’s 18k Gold Testing Safety Kit! narrative: Safely test the karat content of gold with ToolTreaux’s 18k Gold Testing Safety Kit! The kit includes x1 0.5oz shatterproof plastic squeeze bottle, x1 pair of safety glasses, x2 plastic pipettes, x2 glass vials and x1 testing stone; the 18k gold testing solution will see you through hundreds of tests! A must-have tool for gold panning, coin, antiques and jewelry appraisal, or other professions that work with gold and precious metals. 18k GOLD TESTING KIT METHOD: This gold testing method is ideally used on gold that is yet to be smelted, due to the harsh nature of the solution that could potentially damage an already finished item; if testing fine jewelry or other finished gold items, be sure to choose an inconspicuous spot so as not to compromise the finish of the item HOW THE GOLD TESTING KIT WORKS: To test gold karat content, scratch the item onto the test stone leaving a visible deposit, then place a small drop of the solution over top of deposit; if the gold is the same karat or higher the solution will not affect the deposit; if the deposit is of a lower karat, the deposit will dissolve and eventually disappear; ensure gold testing stone has been thoroughly washed before completing any testing SAFETY NOTE: Testing Solutions can be harmful to the skin. Please exercise the utmost caution when opening and using this product and method of testing! Proper protective eye wear and gloves are highly recommended, as the solution can burn unprotected skin. Read the label on the bottle and follow the included operational instructions.

short_description: Safely test for platinum purity with ToolTreaux’s Platinum Purity Testing Safety Kit! The kit includes x1 0.5oz shatterproof plastic squeeze bottle, x1 pair of safety glasses, x2 plastic pipettes, x2 glass vials and x1 testing stone; the platinum purity testing solution will see you through hundreds of tests! A must-have tool for gold panning, coin collectors, antiques and jewelry appraisal, or other professions that work with precious metals.
narrative: Safely test for silver purity with ToolTreaux’s Silver Purity Testing Safety Kit! The kit includes x1 0.5oz shatterproof plastic squeeze bottle, x1 pair of safety glasses, x2 plastic pipettes, x2 glass vials and x1 testing stone; the silver purity testing solution will see you through hundreds of tests! A must-have tool for gold panning, coin, antiques and jewelry appraisal, or other professions that work with precious metals. SILVER TESTING KIT METHOD: This silver testing method is ideally used on material that is yet to be smelted, due to the harsh nature of the solution that could potentially damage an already finished item; if testing fine jewelry or other finished silver or potentially sterling silver items, be sure to choose an inconspicuous spot so as not to compromise the finish of the item TESTING FOR SILVER: To test for silver purity and quality, scratch the item onto the thoroughly washed test stone, leaving a visible deposit, then place a small drop of the solution over top of deposit; if the material is of high quality silver, the solution will turn bright red; if the solution turns blue or green the sample may contain brass or copper; if the sample deposit dissolves quickly, or the solution turns a dark brown or yellow, it is likely a base metal (or could potentially be a silver plated item in which case results can vary depending on deposit) SAFETY NOTE: Testing Solutions can be harmful to the skin. Please exercise the utmost caution when opening and using this product and method of testing! Proper protective eye wear and gloves are highly recommended, as the solution can burn unprotected skin. Read the label on the bottle and follow the included operational instructions.

narrative: Have you ever found yourself in a situation where you needed emergency supplies but didn't have any on hand? These situations can range in urgency from a child needing a bandage for a small cut or scrape to helping someone who is badly hurt and there are no available first responders. To address this, we've put together the ASR Outdoor 100 Piece First Aid Kit containing supplies to help you treat minor injuries and wounds, which will provide some peace of mind and ensure you have the ability to respond quickly until medical professionals can arrive. Inside of the first response kit, you'll find supplies for cleaning and protecting wounds as well as tools to assist in your first response efforts. The cleaning supplies included are 10 alcohol wipes, 4 sting relief wipes, soap wipes, iodine prep pad, and cotton swabs. To protect the wound once it's been thoroughly cleaned, we've also included 20 medium-sized bandages, 15 large bandages, 5 mini bandages, 5 circular bandages, 1 butterfly bandage, 1 elastic gauze bandage, 1 moleskin sheet, 1 eye pad, 2 burn gels, 1 emergency blanket, and 1 fever cooling patch. These different sized bandages along with the additional supplies should be able to address the most common minor injuries such as a cut, scrape, burn or blister. To help you properly clean and protect wounds, we've included these tools: 6 safety pins, 1 pair of gloves, 1 CPR mask, 1 tweezer, 1 medical tape roll, 1 EMT scissor, and 1 TPE tourniquet. These tools will help you dress wounds and stop or slow blood flow of injuries until first responders can arrive. It's important to use a CPR mask when giving CPR so that you can protect yourself as well as the injured individual. In addition to all of these first aid supplies, we've included a bright red waterproof dry sack that will protect the supplies in the event that the bag becomes submerged or affected by inclement weather. The high-visibility red color will help you locate the kit in poor lighting conditions like in the trunk of a car or if an emergency happens at night. The entire kit measures just 7.5" x 5" x 3" and weighs only .6 pounds (9.6 ounces), making it a great size for keeping in your car, boat, bug out bag, camping gear or backpack while traveling. There are many reasons to keep a first aid kit nearby, including liability for businesses and employees. When an injury occurs, a first aid kit can save lives and keep you prepared for an accident, which we all know are unexpected. No one wants to think about being sued, and businesses by law are required to keep first aid supplies readily available when there is no first response within 10 minutes.
